item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Saturday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
gives you 15 intelligence and also partial invisibility
we've all been there
ok
yes it does
cocaine?
yes please
accurate
actually was pretty normal then, i'm weird now
tired of them asking that
i'm a guy and i want to be many of these
as is tradition
mmm hmm
i noticed
classic Frank
finally
and with that..
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: We're staring down the barrel of 4 years of hell.
item.description: As a military veteran, I can't begin to tell you how much it eats me up inside that the orange shitstain, that we'd hoped we'd successfully wiped off, has come back ready to end democracy as we know it. I've got a ton I wanna say, but I sincerely want to thank our(at the moment, still) Allied forces. I served with many of you during deployment back in 2008 and I want to say that despite what Cheeto Mousilini will surely say/do, you are and will always be allies and friends in my book! ??
item.dc:creator: ironman3010

item.title: Thunderstorm Supercell
item.description: "Inside the supercell, some of the low-level winds converge and curve, circulating and creating a rotating updraft of the supercell thunderstorm. Called the mesocyclone, this rotating updraft forms within the storm." Source: https://www.foxweather.com/learn/what-is-a-supercell-thunderstorm
item.dc:creator: Fulustreka

item.title: 10th Cakeday Dump: Punk Edition
item.description:
My 10th Favorite sets the theme for this dump. He's not wrong, baby loves a bad boy who can't afford to get on the bus.
RIP Biquette AKA Biquette de Mauriac, the Grindcore Goat or the Punk Rock Goat (2003-2013).
Fuck the Klan.
Tack MAGA onto the list. Conservatives aren't punk, go home.
Politics and punk, so inseparable that everyone still hates Reagan.
Punks in the USSR, circa 1980-something.
Retracing and commemorating yesterday's punk at the Palladium
Fuck the cops too while you're at it
Maybe yes, maybe no.
Painfully accurate, no pun intended.
Is it any surprise that Belushi loved Black Flag too? A man of culture and distinction.
Do good, look rad, please don't throw up on the carpet.
The legendary Louie Armstrong AKA "Satchmo" loved punk, a talented man of great taste.
Goth-adjacent, June and July are marathons of dehydration. No, I will not improve the situation by laying off the liquor.
item.dc:creator: circlebreaker

item.description:
As a cat owner who wears a lot of black...yeah
I am here to make an announcement
Destroying Elon Musk's Testicles with a spinning kick is a wholesome traditional family activity.
holy fucking shit man
thank you hunter :)
shikanoko nokonoko koshitantan
Who's gonna tell them.
Duncan and Eddie is a great comic.
Americans going on RedNote
Monsters. The answer is usually Monsters.
b a g
http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging http://bsky.abbii.gay: For Bluesky http://onlyfans.abbii.gay: for Tips! Hello mortals. So I'm posting this on the 19th. Tomorrow in the US is Inaugaration day. Shit's gonna get wild and rough over there and, I suspect, a lot of other places as well. Look out for each other, help eachother, and be defiant. Make things very awkward for these cunts. Shit's gonna get wild and everyone being scattered is what they want to happen.

item.title: The new defence secretary of the United States. Utter bonehead.
item.description: I think this twat might’ve once heard a scientist say that germs are not an actual thing, which I guess is kind of true? What he might not have realized is that there are either bacteria or viruses, and the word “germs” are more of a colloquialism that include both of those. And likely the most effective way of curtailing their spread is to wash your hands. Wankstar.
item.dc:creator: CALAMOSCOPYJANE

item.title: $25 / bottle of water. My friend texted me,do not even lift anything from there.I saw his text after … it was too late
item.description: I called the receptionist, we both were laughing. He said yes , if you lift anything up from there it will charge you. I see 24.99 usd . He said even you go buy same bottle of water and put it back from CVS still charge will be there. He removed the charge. Thank you my receptionist After that I bought 3 lt water x 3 = $12 from CVS. It was hard to carry to hotel. But I will not pay 25 usd per 1 lt water :)
item.dc:creator: Balikadam

item.title: Should billionaires even exist?
item.description: EXTREME WEALTH EQUALS EXTREME POWER Concentrated power in the hands of the few undermines democratic institutions and popular control. Modern capitalism grants outsized influence to wealthy elites, allowing them to shape economic policy, public opinion, and cultural norms. Billionaires, by definition, embody the most extreme form of concentrated wealth and power. This level of wealth does not merely exist in a vacuum: it comes with the capacity to dominate media narratives, lobby governments, and steer public policy in their favor, virtually always at the expense of the population. Democracy ideally operates on the principle of one person, one vote. But in a society where a billionaire’s political contributions can dwarf those of thousands or even millions of ordinary citizens, the principle of political equality is brutally compromised. When billionaires use their resources to sway electoral outcomes, shape legislation, or influence public discourse, they effectively silence or dilute the voice of those with fewer resources. THE INHERENT EXPLOITATION OF ACCUMULATING BILLIONS The capitalist system isn't just about "free enterprise" (I'd argue it never was). Rather, it’s riddled with structures of dominance that funnel wealth upward. Workers create value, but the profits disproportionately benefit owners and shareholders, ultimately boosting the net worth of the wealthiest far beyond any measure of direct personal contribution. The very existence of billionaires is symptomatic of an economic system that enables vast surpluses to flow to a minuscule elite. This is elementary logic even a child could understand, no one individual’s labor or 'genius' alone can account for such an astronomical fortune. Instead, it reflects a system that permits and encourages the extraction of wealth/value from workers on an obscenely gargantuan scale. For a society that prides itself on fair rewards for hard work, the gap between the wealthiest and the average worker suggests a distortion of labor compensation. If workers had genuine bargaining power, profit sharing, or ownership stakes, extreme wealth gaps would diminish. Resource Misallocation and Societal Distortions THE “FREE MARKET” MYTHOLOGY So-called “free markets” are anything but free; they’re propped up by corporate subsidies, favorable legislation, and unequal enforcement of laws. Billionaires thrive in these environments, that allow them to take advantage of tax loopholes, offshore accounts, and preferential regulations. The cost of these privileges is often carried by everyday people—through cuts to public services, rising public debt, and underfunded social programs. When large sums of wealth remain in a few hands instead of circulating in the broader economy, resources are not used in ways that maximize public good. Large fortunes often flow into speculative investments or lavish consumption (mega-yachts, mansions, private jets), which benefits a tiny slice of the population. Meanwhile, investments in healthcare, education, and infrastructure, which could elevate society as a whole, remain underfunded. AMPLIFIED INEQUALITY HARMS SOCIAL COHESION Severe inequality erodes trust in institutions and fosters cynicism, which in turn can nurture authoritarian tendencies. It also drives division across racial, ethnic, and class lines, as groups struggle over limited resources. Billionaires perpetuate structures that exacerbate these inequalities via political lobbying, controlling media outlets, or championing anti-labor legislation. When a society becomes overly stratified, empathy declines. The extreme gap between the billionaire class and the average person can create a sense of hopelessness or resentment, sometimes fueling dangerous populist responses (though perhaps that's just what we need right now?). TL;DR The existence of billionaires is not merely a quirk of the market. It is the manifestation of deeply entrenched power structures that subvert democracy and exploit our labor. Such immense personal wealth inherently undermines social and economic justice. Addressing this issue requires concerted political action, grassroots mobilization, and systemic reforms targeting the root causes of wealth concentration. By embracing progressive policies, fostering equitable labor practices, and demanding accountability, we can push back against a system that currently allows for the existence of billionaires. And, in doing so, work toward a society grounded in fairness, democracy, and the collective well-being of every one of us, even the self-destructive human drones involved in personality cults like 'maga'.
item.dc:creator: NChomsky
